findings.title: ACT-prompt-amendment assertion is weakened by a spoon-fed prompt
findings.description: The §22 A2 amended criterion line ('...or a status/information query addressed to a named callsign') is correctly present in HOT_LOOP_PROMPT, but the same prompt also explicitly instructs the model 'For the status query choose panopticon.steer ... For pass-1 choose observe.pass.' assertActPromptAmendment would therefore pass even if the amended criterion were removed, so it does not isolate the amendment's classification effect. Acceptable for a reachability/latency probe whose verdict is red anyway, but the amendment is not independently proven.

findings.title: Determinism is decision-identity, not byte-identical temp-0
findings.description: assertDeterministic compares canonicalDecisions (id/decision/tool/arguments) and excludes confidence/reason, which do vary across the two same-input runs (selected claude candidate: pass-1 confidence 0.93 vs 0.92). This is a reasonable and documented record-replay invariant (routing action is identical) and is the right choice for subscription CLIs, but it is explicitly weaker than strict byte-identical temperature-0 output. Documented in RESULT.md.

findings.title: Planning-doc tension on whether A-LLM-SUB is blocking
findings.description: 03-eng §22 A2.4 / §17 / §20 / R15 declare A-LLM-SUB 'MOOT/removed in V0' and the hot-loop model choice 'a tunable, not a blocking PRD §6 conflict', while 04-backpressure rows 193/194/219 and this ticket treat P-LLM/A-LLM-SUB as blocking with a binding PRD §6 conflict. The implementer correctly followed the ticket + backpressure gate rows (the binding-conflict path). Flagged for the gate to reconcile the docs; not an implementation defect.

findings.title: Cost gate recorded as unmeasured rather than treated as flat-rate zero
findings.description: estimatedCostPerHourUsd returns null for host-subscription routes (no per-call metering), so costWithinBudget is false and becomes a blocker. This is the conservative, intellectually honest choice (treating unmetered subscription access as $0/hr would make the $0.15/hr gate tautological) and is consistent with 'surface the conflict, do not engineer around it'. Noted as a deliberate, defensible decision, not a defect.
